surface_verification.justification: Backend-affects-Frontend: server/src/core/jsonl-records.ts was split (decoding primitives extracted to jsonl-decode.ts), and that module is the record reader behind the Triage/Inbox board, the event-log projections and campaign-events — code consumed by the UI — so surface=web applies even though no client file changed. Executed client/e2e/flows/triage-record-boundary-recovery.spec.ts + campaign-events-projection.spec.ts in real Chromium against an isolated single-process stack (temp USERPROFILE, built client via SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR, Hono 127.0.0.1:3849, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local): 2 passed. Verified LOAD-BEARING by re-running against a deliberately broken jsonl-decode.ts (decodeObjectAt returning null, killing slow-path recovery): the recovery flow FAILED as required (trg-aaaa0001 never rendered), proving the spec exercises the extracted code through the UI rather than passing incidentally; restored and re-verified green.

behaviors.evidence: server jsonl-records.test.ts + jsonl-records.faults.test.ts + event-log-recovery.test.ts — 91/91 unchanged and green

behaviors.behavior: Consumers keep working across the split (no import or symbol change)
behaviors.evidence: event-log-reader.test.ts, triage-store.test.ts, run-data-join.file.test.ts green; full server suite 2894 passed; tsc --noEmit clean both workspaces

behaviors.behavior: Record recovery still works end-to-end in the UI
behaviors.evidence: E2E triage-record-boundary-recovery.spec.ts + campaign-events-projection.spec.ts, real Chromium vs real server, 2/2; proven load-bearing by a deliberate break that made it FAIL

behaviors.behavior: Recorded totals count executed tests, not collected ones
behaviors.evidence: new server/src/test/event-test-counts-executed.test.ts (4 assertions); non-vacuous by an explicit in-scope guard, and it CAUGHT a real offender (evt-0e22eb2f) that D4 could not see

behaviors.behavior: A malformed tests block fails the guard instead of being skipped
behaviors.evidence: same guard, 'records a well-formed tests block' — asserts non-negative integers rather than filtering non-numbers (external review finding)

behaviors.behavior: tests.skipped stays a non-negative integer, apart from the executed total
B7: 20 commits relinked to their events
behaviors.evidence: audit re-run B7 pass (65 commits, 52 matched); pre-write guards refuse a duplicate, unreachable or already-linked target

behaviors.behavior: B7: PR #276's missing event recorded retroactively and schema-valid
behaviors.evidence: written via record_event.py, whose FR + spec_impact gates rejected two earlier drafts; audit re-run keeps D5 pass

behaviors.behavior: D2: the fold is scoped by a spec_updated watermark
behaviors.evidence: audit re-run D2 pass; measured 78 stale refs before the regroup, 0 after; watermark verified to take the target's ts, not write time

behaviors.behavior: D1/D3: promised FRs read as delivered
behaviors.evidence: audit re-run D1 + D3 pass; all 8 FRs confirmed by their delivering commit naming the FR

behaviors.behavior: D4: every same-cause shortfall corrected, not only the flagged ones
behaviors.evidence: audit re-run D4 pass; 30 records corrected under a two-condition guard (delta==1 AND after the symlink test landed); 3 deliberately refused and named

behaviors.behavior: G2: every scope in active use resolves
behaviors.evidence: audit re-run G2 pass over 65 commits; the 18 scopes were taken verbatim from the audit's own G2 evidence

behaviors.behavior: F5: arch-impact drops documented in their target doc
behaviors.evidence: audit re-run F5 pass; bullet <=600-char rule re-checked and check_agent_doc_budget.py OK

behaviors.behavior: H1/H2: baseline matches disk and holds real code to the rule
behaviors.evidence: audit re-run H0-H6 pass; jsonl-records.ts 317->251 and deliberately NOT added to the baseline; update script refuses any loosening

behaviors.behavior: C1's recorded reason is legible to the next reader
behaviors.reason: whether prose persuades the next reader not to silence the check is a human judgment; the mechanical half (C1 absent from disabled_checks, still reported) IS asserted by the audit re-run

behaviors.behavior: An amendment preserves every original tests key (e2e_run, new, modified)
behaviors.evidence: internal review found the shallow-merge data loss (28 records lost e2e_run, 6 of them true); repaired and re-verified through the real consumer apply_amendments — 0 records still losing keys, 60 keep e2e_run:true

behaviors.behavior: The guard reads the event log the way the audit does (concatenation-tolerant)
behaviors.evidence: effectiveEvents() now goes through parseJsonlRecords (the module this iterate split) instead of a per-line JSON.parse, so a merge=union concatenation cannot hide an offender from the guard while the audit still judges it; guard 4/4 green
Date.parse instant comparison replaces the lexical one; 70 records in this log carry +02:00 rather than Z, so string order != chronological order

behaviors.behavior: The three A-campaign backfills do not assert a skip-free run
behaviors.evidence: git cat-file confirmed write-symlink-guard.test.ts existed at 8fbc0a4e / 7e8c97c5 / 4beaf596, so executed = quoted - 1 with skipped:1; recorded with that reasoning in backfill_reason
